page_content:
HEBR203 Intermediate Hebrew II-A (3 Credits) Continuation of HEBR211 Study of linguistic structure, further development of audio-lingual, reading, writing, and speaking skills. Reading of texts and newspapers designed to give some knowledge of Hebrew life, thought and culture. Corresponds to the ﬁrst half of HEBR212. Prerequisite: HEBR212; or must have placement by the Hebrew coordinator. Restriction: Must not have completed HEBR212. Credit Only Granted for: (HEBR203 and HEBR204) or HEBR212.

HEBR298B Introduction to the Hebrew Bible/Old Testament (3 Credits) Origins of the Hebrew Bible (Old Testament), with attention to literary formations, archaeology, and social-political settings. Explorations of major questions, including who wrote the Bible, and when; relationships of the biblical tradition to the mythology and religious structures of ancient Israel's near eastern neighbors; and dynamics of politics, religious leadership, and law. Cross-listed with: JWST262, ENGL262. Credit Only Granted for: JWST262, HEBR298B, or ENGL262.
